scenario: Iris Marketplace must start a new process instance whenever an external system publishes a customerOnboarded message via the broker, with 4000 messages per hour in Camunda 8.8. Each message must create a fresh process instance and seed it with the message payload as process variables.
question: Which event element starts a new process instance whenever a correlated message arrives at the broker?

options.text: Message start event configured with the message name customerOnboarded creates a new process instance on every published correlated message
Message intermediate catch event configured with the message name customerOnboarded creates a new process instance for each published message
Message boundary event attached to a parent process configured with the message name creates a new instance whenever the message is published
Message end event in a separate trigger process publishes the message which then implicitly creates a new instance of the target process

a.text: Correct. A message start event creates a fresh process instance for every published message that matches the configured message name.

b.text: Incorrect. Message intermediate catch events occur inside an already-running process instance; they cannot create a new instance from an external message.

c.text: Incorrect. Boundary events attach to activities of a running instance and never create a new top-level process instance from outside.

d.text: Incorrect. Message end events publish messages; the start-instance behaviour is the role of the receiving process's message start event.

explanation: Message start events are the documented BPMN element that the broker uses to create a new process instance for each correlated incoming message.
